prerequisites: An active Leadfeeder account with access to the data you want to extract. The API key inherits the same permissions as the user who created it, so it can read every Leadfeeder account that user can view.
A start date, used by the connector as the earliest point from which to begin pulling data.

authMethods.name: API token (api_token)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Leadfeeder API key passed as the connector's api_token secret. Leadfeeder sends this key in the x-api-key request header. The token is shown in full only once at creation, so copy and store it securely; most endpoints also require an account_id, derived from the accounts your user can access.

setupSteps.title: Create a Leadfeeder API key
setupSteps.body: Log into Leadfeeder and open your settings (user icon top-right, or the settings gear in the left navigation). Under Personal settings, select API keys, click Create a new API key, and give it a name. The token is generated immediately. You can only see the full token once, so copy it right away and store it in a safe place.

setupSteps.title: Provide the api_token
setupSteps.body: Paste the API key you created into the connector's api_token field. This is a required secret. Leadfeeder authenticates requests by sending it in the x-api-key header, and the key grants access to every account your Leadfeeder user can view.

setupSteps.title: Set the start_date
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date field to the earliest date from which the connector should begin retrieving data. Records before this date are not synced.

sources.title: How to Create and Use Your Leadfeeder API Keys
API Key and Permissions - Leadfeeder Help Centre
